title: Put Your Head On My Shoulders

jokes.text: Four score and 1145 years ago... our forefathers' foreheads conceived a new nation
jokes.setup: Parody of Lincoln's Gettysburg Address opening
jokes.punchline: Adding 1145 years and 'foreheads' instead of 'fathers'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - historical parody with sci-fi twist and visual pun on Nixon's head/forehead

jokes.text: I'm Malfunctioning Eddie... and I'm malfunctioning so badly, I'm giving these cars away!
jokes.setup: Car salesman introduces himself as 'Malfunctioning Eddie'
jokes.punchline: His malfunction is being TOO generous to customers
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - his 'malfunction' subverts expectations about sleazy car salesmen

jokes.text: My parents said if I got B's, they'd buy me a bar. I got all C's!
jokes.setup: Parents promised reward for good grades
jokes.punchline: She failed to meet the standard but still expects reward
jokes.explanation: Establishes Amy's privileged, entitled character while subverting grade expectations

jokes.text: Mind if I tag along? My ass needs servicing
jokes.setup: Asking to join a car shopping trip
jokes.punchline: Double entendre - robot butt literally needs mechanical service
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character integration - only works because Bender is a robot with mechanical parts

jokes.text: No wonder you're at the end of conga lines
jokes.setup: Fry reading about car safety issues
jokes.punchline: Dry observation connecting car problems to social positioning
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor with good visual imagery

jokes.text: This is a Beta Romeo. Yes, the Beta Romeo
jokes.setup: Car salesman introducing a car model
jokes.punchline: Play on 'Alpha Romeo' but with 'Beta' suggesting inferiority
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective car brand parody

jokes.text: Note the cross-your-heart seat belt. It protects, lifts and separates
jokes.punchline: Combining seat belt function with bra advertising slogans
jokes.explanation: Creative mashup of automotive and lingerie marketing

jokes.text: Yeah, beautiful coffee. No, the Ford Thunder Cougar Falcon Bird!
jokes.setup: Victor assumes Fry is admiring a woman
jokes.punchline: Fry was literally looking at coffee, then absurdly long car name
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection followed by memorably ridiculous car name

jokes.text: I see you don't care if people question your sexual orientation
jokes.setup: Fry says he's not buying a car
jokes.punchline: Aggressive salesmanship using toxic masculinity
jokes.explanation: Parodies aggressive car sales tactics and toxic masculinity

jokes.text: I do, but I don't know how to make them stop!
jokes.setup: Victor's aggressive sales pitch about masculinity
jokes.punchline: Fry's pathetic, honest response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry response - earnest insecurity that's both funny and relatable

jokes.text: The seats are stuffed with eagle down... the dash inlaid with beaks of eagles. There are eagles under the floorboards
jokes.punchline: Absurd escalation of eagle-based components
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure building to absurd extremes

jokes.text: What's wrong? The luxury edition has more eagle
jokes.punchline: There's somehow an even more eagle-heavy version
jokes.explanation: Continues the absurd escalation to logical extreme

jokes.text: I installed bumpers to reduce the risk of butt failure
jokes.punchline: Technical terminology applied to robot anatomy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor Farnsworth pseudo-scientific terminology

jokes.text: You, sir, have defaced a national treasure! I demand you restore my buttocks to their former glory!
jokes.setup: Bender upset about his rear modifications
jokes.punchline: Treating his robot butt as a 'national treasure'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender ego and vanity about his mechanical parts

jokes.text: All right, but that ass is gonna blow, and when it does... I pray you're not mooning someone you care about
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about literal explosion and 'mooning'
jokes.explanation: Excellent layered wordplay that works on multiple levels

jokes.text: No dog food for Victor tonight!
jokes.punchline: Reveals his pathetic living situation
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy revealing the car salesman's desperate situation

jokes.text: Sixty thousand! ... I thought it was an auction
jokes.setup: Amy offering more than the asking price
jokes.punchline: Fry's confusion about basic sales concepts
jokes.explanation: Shows both Amy's wealth and Fry's cluelessness about commerce

jokes.text: The sticker says $55,000. But we'll only go as high as, say... Sixty thousand!
jokes.setup: Amy negotiating car price, sticker says $55k
jokes.punchline: She offers MORE than asking price - bad at negotiating
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Amy's wealth and complete lack of business sense. Good structure.

jokes.text: He is not too happy. I'm sorry. Eighty thousand?
jokes.punchline: Amy offering even MORE money
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation of Amy's financial cluelessness

jokes.text: You're asking for a day off? Get out of my sight! ... I asked me for Valentine's Day off. I was in no mood for my shenanigans
jokes.setup: Hermes appearing to yell at someone
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was yelling at himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - bureaucrat so rigid he denies himself time off

jokes.text: Valentine's Day's coming? I forgot to get a girlfriend again
jokes.punchline: Treating girlfriend acquisition like a routine task he forgot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - treating relationships like errands

jokes.text: The usual. Boiling lead, lava. So, what? Shorts?
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual response about appropriate attire
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to extreme conditions, great economy

jokes.text: Boy, this a/c is incredible. I better turn on the heater too
jokes.punchline: Immediately wanting to counteract it with heat
jokes.explanation: Shows Amy's wasteful rich kid mentality

jokes.text: This heater is incredible! I better turn up the a/c some more
jokes.punchline: Escalating the wasteful cycle
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that escalates the wasteful behavior

jokes.text: I'm in the Astro-Afro-Antarctico-Amerasian Auto Association
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously long acronym organization name
jokes.explanation: Parodies AAA with absurdly inclusive naming

jokes.text: You're rich. How come you dress like you're doing your laundry?
jokes.punchline: Specific comparison to laundry day attire
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about wealth and appearance

jokes.text: My folks were always on me to wear underpants. What am I, the pope?
jokes.setup: Parents wanting him to wear underwear
jokes.punchline: Comparing underwear to papal vestments
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic connecting mundane and sacred

jokes.text: If you were, they'd be all, 'Straighten your pope hat' and 'Put on your good vestments'
jokes.punchline: Amy extending the metaphor with specific papal complaints
jokes.explanation: Good callback that builds on Fry's absurd comparison

jokes.text: It's nice to find someone I can talk to about stuff, and junk. It's like we feel the same way about junk and stuff, or whatever
jokes.punchline: Vague, inarticulate way of expressing deep feelings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy character voice - genuine feelings expressed poorly

jokes.text: While they tow us, you wanna do it?
jokes.punchline: Casual suggestion of sex during towing
jokes.explanation: Amy's casual attitude toward sex creates awkward moment

jokes.text: You snagged the perfect girlfriend. Amy's rich, she's probably got other characteristics...
jokes.punchline: Implying her wealth is the only quality worth mentioning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - reducing relationships to material gain

jokes.text: Please! For your information, it's because he's hideous
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty about Zoidberg's appearance
jokes.explanation: Characteristic Bender cruelty delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: $500 and time served. Stupid anti-pimping laws
jokes.punchline: Casual complaint about anti-pimping legislation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - treating illegal activity as minor inconvenience

jokes.text: Honey, we love you! Shut up, baby, I know it!
jokes.punchline: Bender's signature arrogant response
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender catchphrase in perfect context

jokes.text: Computer dating. Like pimping, but you rarely say 'upside your head'
jokes.punchline: Connecting dating service to pimping with specific verbal difference
jokes.explanation: Clever comparison with specific linguistic distinction

jokes.text: Why would anyone ask you for help? Don't make me go upside your head!
jokes.punchline: Immediate demonstration of the pimping language
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback proving the point

jokes.text: If you disrupt the mating dance... the male will maul us with his fearsome gonad
jokes.setup: Warning about interrupting Fry and Amy
jokes.punchline: Nature documentary language applied to human sexuality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant nature documentary parody with absurd terminology

jokes.text: Check off what you look for in a mate. Let's see. Oh, yes. Yes, definitely. I'd like some of that. I'll just have everything on the menu
jokes.punchline: Treating it like ordering from a restaurant menu
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp Brannigan horniness and greed

jokes.text: Okay, Mister...? Smith. Zapp Smith. Brannigan.
jokes.setup: Bender asking for name for dating service
jokes.punchline: Zapp tries to give fake name but can't help saying his real name
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp ego moment - can't even maintain a fake identity because he loves his real name too much.

jokes.text: Wow, we're great kissers
jokes.punchline: Awkward self-congratulation during intimacy
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward Fry moment ruining romance

jokes.text: It was great. But now she's talking about hanging out. She's getting way too serious
jokes.punchline: Treating casual hanging out as overly serious commitment
jokes.explanation: Perfect male commitment phobia taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: I do my job, there's Amy. I go to the candy machine, there's Amy. I sleep with Amy, there's Amy
jokes.punchline: Final item reveals the obvious reason she's there
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation building to obvious but hilarious conclusion

jokes.text: Face it, I'm a prize catch. I'm pulling down delivery boy money
jokes.punchline: Citing his terrible job as evidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry delusion - treating minimum wage as attractive

jokes.text: She has billions in a trust fund. She wants a trophy husband
jokes.punchline: Seeing himself as trophy material despite being a delivery boy
jokes.explanation: Continues Fry's delusional self-importance

jokes.text: I haven't eaten since Tuesday. Bird eggs. Animal slices. Dry woven reeds
jokes.punchline: Clinical descriptions of common picnic foods
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg alien perspective on human food

jokes.text: Pardon me. Excuse me. I'll just turn the wheel to maximum fastness
jokes.punchline: Polite demeanor followed by dangerous driving terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg - overly polite then immediately reckless

jokes.text: Pardon me. Excuse me. I'll just turn the wheel to maximum fastness.
jokes.punchline: 'Maximum fastness' - childish/alien way to describe speed
jokes.explanation: Good character voice - Zoidberg's alien/childish way of describing things. 'Maximum fastness' is quotable.

jokes.text: You know how you like chocolate but get tired of it... when it always wants to hang out? You don't like chocolate? Can chocolate let me finish?
jokes.setup: Fry trying to break up using chocolate metaphor
jokes.punchline: Amy missing the metaphor entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward breakup conversation with failed metaphor

jokes.text: And the winner of the big car race is Hot Rod Zoidberg!
jokes.punchline: Self-congratulation during apparent crash
jokes.explanation: Zoidberg's oblivious celebration during disaster

jokes.text: Your body was badly damaged in the crash. How badly? That's it over there
jokes.setup: Fry asking about his body's condition
jokes.punchline: Professor casually pointing to severed body
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy with casual delivery of horrifying news

jokes.text: Looks like we'll be spending a lot more time together, Fry
jokes.setup: Fry's head attached to Amy's body
jokes.punchline: Amy's ironic observation about their forced intimacy
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - forced together right after breakup

jokes.text: Looks like we'll be spending a lot more time together, Fry.
jokes.setup: Fry wanted to break up with Amy for being clingy
jokes.punchline: Now his head is attached to her body - ultimate clinginess
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic reversal - Fry's worst fear realized literally. The situation perfectly mirrors the relationship problem he was trying to escape.

jokes.text: This always happens with office romances
jokes.setup: Fry's head literally attached to Amy's body
jokes.punchline: Treating surgical attachment as typical office romance drama
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan treating bizarre situation as mundane

jokes.text: I'll try not to interfere in your life. Sorry. I guess I control that arm
jokes.punchline: Immediately interfering through shared body control
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction of promise

jokes.text: With my body, I think you'll only attract one sort of woman
jokes.setup: Fry claiming he can attract women
jokes.punchline: Amy implying her body limits his options to desperate women
jokes.explanation: Amy's self-deprecating humor about the situation

jokes.text: Losers get really desperate around Valentine's Day. Yeah, it's pathetic, all right
jokes.punchline: Customer agreeing while being the desperate loser
jokes.explanation: Good irony with customer not recognizing self-description

jokes.text: Five hundred bucks. Done. Brannigan okay? No! Six hundred
jokes.punchline: Price goes UP when customer rejects Brannigan
jokes.explanation: Good reversal of typical negotiation expectations

jokes.text: Okay, we're done with the bra. Why did you shave? Expecting something to happen with the date?
jokes.punchline: Fry's inappropriate questions about her grooming
jokes.explanation: Awkward intimacy forced by shared body situation

jokes.text: It's deodorant. What does it do?
jokes.punchline: His complete ignorance of basic hygiene products
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry ignorance about basic personal care

jokes.text: 20 minutes? You're ready now!
jokes.setup: Amy telling her date she needs 20 more minutes
jokes.punchline: Fry's male impatience with female preparation time
jokes.explanation: Classic male/female preparation time conflict

jokes.text: I've got a date. She's coming. ... Just like Fry's date. Get it?
jokes.setup: Fry claiming to have a date
jokes.punchline: Bender pointing out the obvious joke about shared body
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about the situation, slightly forced

jokes.text: How's them eats? Don't mind if I do. ... She seems old. She's well-traveled. I don't mean she travels
jokes.punchline: Bender's euphemism about her sexual experience
jokes.explanation: Bender's crude euphemism about the elderly woman

jokes.text: Wheels fell off my house. Rose? Five bucks. Me too. Eight bucks. You said... Demand skyrocketed. You all saw it
jokes.punchline: Immediate price increase based on 'demand'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation parody of supply/demand economics

jokes.text: Nice eyeball, Eyeball. Nice ass, Ass
jokes.punchline: Crude nicknames based on physical features
jokes.explanation: Crude but character-appropriate construction worker talk

jokes.text: I gotta catch my bus back to Nutley. I'd kiss you but I lost my teeth
jokes.punchline: Multiple pathetic details about her situation
jokes.explanation: Pathetic details that make the bad date worse

jokes.text: Did you round up our dates at the bus station? Of course not. Anybody else for Nutley?
jokes.setup: Leela questioning source of their dates
jokes.punchline: Bus driver immediately proving her suspicion right
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing of suspicion confirmation

jokes.text: Banking industry regulator. Really? I heard that banking industry regulations are very simple. That's not true
jokes.punchline: Leela's obvious attempt to bore Amy's date away
jokes.explanation: Leela's obvious sabotage using boring topics

jokes.text: Modern banking regulations are a product of five regulatory traditions. Six, if you want to get technical. Oh, I do
jokes.punchline: Leela encouraging him to be even more boring
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the boring topic sabotage

jokes.text: There, good as new. Except your dorsal fin. I couldn't find it after the crash. I'll live without it? You call that living?
jokes.punchline: Suggesting missing dorsal fin is catastrophic loss
jokes.explanation: Absurd importance placed on anatomical feature humans don't have

jokes.text: Why wouldn't you let me graft a laser cannon on your chest? To crush those who disobey you
jokes.punchline: Casual suggestion of tyrannical laser weapon
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor Farnsworth - casual suggestions of evil modifications

jokes.text: If I ever feel lonely... I'll look at this disfiguring scar and think of you
jokes.punchline: Romantic sentiment about permanent injury
jokes.explanation: Dark romantic comedy - scar as memento

jokes.text: It's not like you set us up with bad dates... just so we'd be together. Didn't I, Leela? Didn't I?
jokes.setup: Fry suggesting Bender didn't manipulate them
jokes.punchline: Bender's proud admission of manipulation
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - unable to resist taking credit for scheming

jokes.text: No! You just corralled some stiffs at the bus station and took our money. True. But in the end, isn't that what Valentine's Day is all about?
jokes.punchline: Bender turning scam into philosophical statement about holiday
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - turning his scam into holiday wisdom

jokes.text: True. But in the end, isn't that what Valentine's Day is all about?
jokes.punchline: Claims it's the true spirit of Valentine's Day - cynical but oddly wise
jokes.explanation: Excellent cynical wisdom from Bender. The suggestion that Valentine's Day is fundamentally about exploitation is darkly insightful.

jokes.text: Watch the neck! My ass! My beautiful...
jokes.punchline: Bender's butt finally exploding as predicted
jokes.explanation: Satisfying payoff to Professor's earlier warning about butt failure

tmdb_overview: Fry's brief relationship with Amy is forcibly prolonged when a near-fatal car crash leaves Fry's head grafted to Amy's left shoulder. Meanwhile, Bender cashes in on Valentine's Day by starting a dating service.
